title: status
description: Live project state — the 'where are we' source of truth. Agents read this first each session; humans view it via the dashboard. Keep it current: a stale status is worse than none. It is a snapshot, not a journal — git history is the fine-grained log.

oneLiner.description: One sentence: what the project is.

lastUpdated.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) this file was last updated. Bump on every change.

now.description: What is actively in flight right now. A short list of current focus, not a backlog.

next.description: What comes immediately after Now. Near-term, not the whole roadmap.

milestones.description: Bigger checkpoints. Flip `done` to true when reached.

title.description: Milestone name, e.g. 'M1 — first usable slice'.

done.description: Whether this milestone has been reached.

target.description: Optional target date or period, e.g. '2026-07'. Empty string if none.

blocked.description: Active blockers and open questions only — remove an entry once it's resolved.

since.description: Optional ISO date it became a blocker.

shipped.description: Completed work, newest first. A dated snapshot log of meaningful units of work — not the full git history.

commit.description: Optional commit SHA. Empty string if none.

link.description: Optional URL (PR, commit, doc). Empty string if none.
